channel.title: Markets News Today
channel.description: Breaking news and expert analysis on business, finance, technology and world affairs.

item.title: Sam Altman Sorry for Not Alerting Police to School Shooter's AI Use
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has issued a public apology after it emerged that the company failed to alert authorities about the ChatGPT account of a gunman who killed eight people at a British Columbia school. The shooter's account had been banned approximately eight months before the massacre took place. The admission has reignited fierce debate over the responsibilities of artificial intelligence companies when they detect potentially dangerous users on their platforms.

item.title: 2026 NFL Draft Day 2: Biggest Steals and Reaches Graded
item.description: Day 2 of the 2026 NFL Draft delivered a flurry of picks across rounds two and three, with several franchises securing elite value while others raised eyebrows with questionable selections. Using Pro Football Focus grades and Big Board rankings, analysts have identified clear winners and losers from Friday night's action. Here is a full breakdown of the most significant steals and reaches from the second day of the draft.

item.title: Summer House Cast Reacts to Season 10 Reunion Audio Leak
item.description: The Summer House Season 10 reunion has not yet aired, but audio from the special has already made its way online, sparking reactions from the cast. Kyle Cooke is speaking out, calling the leak "unfortunate" and a "bummer" for everyone involved. The incident raises fresh questions about privacy and security in the world of reality television production.

item.title: Helena Bonham Carter Exits 'The White Lotus' Season 4
item.description: Helena Bonham Carter has unexpectedly exited the cast of The White Lotus Season 4, the highly anticipated continuation of Mike White's acclaimed HBO anthology series. Production is currently underway in France, with the season set against the glamorous backdrop of the Cannes Film Festival. The departure marks a significant shakeup for a season that had already generated enormous buzz.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Hormuz Standoff Deepens as Israel Hits Lebanon
item.description: The United States and Iran remain locked in a tense and costly confrontation over the Strait of Hormuz with no diplomatic resolution in sight. Simultaneously, Israel has launched a new round of airstrikes in Lebanon, straining already fragile ceasefire agreements. Global markets and energy prices are reacting sharply as both crises show no signs of abating.

item.title: Meta Employees React to Pending Job Cuts: '28 Days of Hell'
item.description: Inside Meta, thousands of employees are enduring an excruciating period of uncertainty as the company prepares to announce significant layoffs. Workers have described the weeks-long limbo as emotionally and professionally devastating, with many unsure whether they will still have jobs by the end of May 2026.

item.title: DOJ Brings Back Firing Squads for Federal Executions
item.description: The Department of Justice has announced sweeping measures to strengthen the federal death penalty, including the reinstatement of firing squads as an approved method of execution. The move marks one of the most significant shifts in federal capital punishment policy in decades. Officials say the changes are designed to ensure death sentences are carried out swiftly and without legal delays.

item.title: Samsung Faces First-Ever Annual Loss in Smartphone Business
item.description: Samsung Electronics is confronting an unprecedented financial crisis as an AI-driven memory shortage squeezes its smartphone division to the brink of its first-ever annual loss. The South Korean tech giant, long considered a global powerhouse in mobile devices, is now grappling with soaring component costs, weakening consumer demand, and fierce competition. Industry analysts warn the situation could reshape the global smartphone market for years to come.

item.title: Meet the 19-Meter Cretaceous Kraken That Swam With Mosasaurs
item.description: Paleontologists have uncovered evidence of a colossal cephalopod predator stretching nearly 19 meters that once ruled the ancient seas alongside mosasaurs during the Cretaceous period. Layer by layer, researchers painstakingly revealed the fossilized jaws of this terrifying creature, rewriting our understanding of apex predators in prehistoric oceans. The discovery is being hailed as one of the most significant marine paleontology finds in decades.

item.title: Witkoff and Kushner to Meet Iran's FM in Pakistan
item.description: Senior US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are expected to meet with Iran's foreign minister in Pakistan in a high-stakes diplomatic push to revive stalled nuclear talks. The meeting comes amid growing fears of military escalation after days of little to no progress in negotiations. Global markets and oil markets are already feeling the strain of rising tensions in the region.

item.title: Spirit Airlines Bailout: What Could Happen Next
item.description: Spirit Airlines finds itself at a critical crossroads as bondholders weigh their options and President Trump floats the possibility of a government purchase of the struggling ultra-low-cost carrier. With restructuring deadlines looming and financial pressure mounting across the aviation sector, the fate of Spirit could be decided in the coming weeks.

item.title: DOJ Drops Powell Inquiry, Clearing Path for Warsh Confirmation
item.description: The Justice Department has officially dropped its inquiry into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, a move widely seen as clearing the political runway for the Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh, President Trump's preferred nominee to lead the central bank. The decision marks a significant turning point in a months-long battle over the independence and leadership of the nation's most powerful financial institution. Markets and economists are now closely watching how a potential Warsh-led Fed could reshape monetary policy at a particularly volatile moment for the global economy.

item.title: Intel Stock Surges 23% After Earnings: Can AI Drive It Higher?
item.description: Intel shares exploded higher on Friday, surging as much as 23% after the chipmaker posted blockbuster first-quarter 2026 results that blew past Wall Street expectations. The rally puts Intel on track for one of its best single-day performances in years, raising a critical question for investors: can artificial intelligence momentum carry the stock even further?

item.title: Body of Missing USF Doctoral Student Found, Roommate Arrested
item.description: The body of a missing University of South Florida doctoral student has been discovered, with his roommate taken into custody Friday following a domestic violence incident at their shared residence. Law enforcement confirmed the grim discovery as the university community reacted with shock and grief. Authorities are continuing their investigation into the circumstances surrounding the young scholar's death.

item.title: Silent Measles Spread Threatens US Elimination Status
item.description: Advanced testing methods are revealing that measles is circulating far more broadly across the United States than official case counts indicate. A landmark investigation into an outbreak along the Arizona-Utah border found the true scope was at least 6.5 times larger than documented. Public health experts now warn that the United States could be at serious risk of losing its hard-won measles elimination status.

item.title: Giant 60-Foot Octopus Ruled Ancient Seas, Fossil Jaws Reveal
item.description: Scientists have uncovered compelling evidence that a kraken-like octopus stretching up to 60 feet in length once dominated prehistoric seas as a top predator. New analyses of fossilized jaw structures are rewriting what researchers thought they knew about ancient marine ecosystems. The discovery places these colossal cephalopods among the most fearsome hunters of the Mesozoic Era.

item.title: Netanyahu Treated for Early-Stage Prostate Cancer
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has been treated for early-stage prostate cancer following the successful removal of a malignant tumour. The 76-year-old leader says he is in excellent physical condition and remains fully engaged in leading the country during a period of significant regional tension.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Roasts Trump in Blistering Alt-WHCD Special
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel turned his late-night platform into a full-scale comedy tribunal on Thursday, delivering what many are calling the most devastating roast of President Donald Trump and his administration in recent television history. Billing it as his own "alternative" White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel spared no one, from the President himself to First Lady Melania Trump and a parade of Cabinet members. The segment has already gone viral, generating millions of views and reigniting debate about the role of comedy in holding political power accountable.

item.title: Intel Stock Surges Toward Best Single Day in Four Decades
item.description: Intel's stock is roaring back to life in dramatic fashion, extending a remarkable rally that could mark the company's best single trading day in approximately four decades. The surge has ignited a fierce debate on Wall Street about whether the semiconductor giant's financial prospects have genuinely turned a corner or whether investors are getting ahead of themselves.

item.title: What the Michael Jackson Movie Deliberately Leaves Out
item.description: The long-awaited Michael Jackson biopic hit theaters on April 24, 2026, but it arrived with significant omissions that have drawn sharp criticism from journalists, documentary filmmakers, and abuse advocates. Scenes that once addressed the 1993 sexual abuse allegations against the pop star were deliberately reshot or removed from the final cut. The result is a film that many say sanitizes one of the most contested legacies in music history.

item.title: Hezbollah Defiant as Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Extended
item.description: Hezbollah declared the extended Israel-Lebanon ceasefire "meaningless" on Friday as Israeli forces carried out fresh strikes across southern Lebanon hours after President Trump announced a prolonged truce. The defiant posture from the militant group signals that a durable peace remains elusive despite diplomatic efforts from Washington. Regional tensions continue to rattle global markets and energy supplies as the conflict shows no sign of abating.

item.title: HPV Vaccine for Men: Why It Matters More Than You Think
item.description: A groundbreaking new study reveals that men who received the HPV vaccine experienced a 46% reduction in their risk of developing HPV-related cancers. Despite this compelling data, vaccination rates among males remain significantly lower than those among females. Medical experts are now urging a critical shift in public perception around who the HPV vaccine is truly for.

item.title: Iranian FM Araghchi Visits Pakistan Amid US Nuclear Talks
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has arrived in Pakistan as Islamabad takes center stage in a renewed push toward US-Iran nuclear diplomacy. The high-stakes visit comes with significant disruption to daily life in the Islamabad-Rawalpindi twin cities, where residents are bearing the economic and logistical weight of the historic talks. The diplomatic development marks one of the most consequential moments in Iran-US relations in years.

item.title: DOJ Drops Criminal Probe of Fed Chair Powell, Clearing Path
item.description: The Department of Justice has officially closed its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, a development that could reshape the future of U.S. monetary policy leadership. The probe had served as a significant obstacle to President Donald Trump's effort to install Kevin Warsh as Powell's successor. Markets are now closely watching what comes next for the central bank and its independence.

item.title: Mike Vrabel Speaks After New Dianna Russini Photos Surface
item.description: New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel addressed reporters at Gillette Stadium on Saturday after a new round of photos surfaced showing him alongside prominent NFL reporter Dianna Russini. Vrabel spoke candidly, acknowledging that his behavior did not reflect the standards he holds himself to both personally and professionally. The statement drew immediate national attention as the NFL community responded to one of its most prominent coaches confronting a personal controversy head-on.

item.title: Two Arrested in Alleged Plot to Attack Houston Synagogue
item.description: Federal authorities have arrested two young individuals in connection with an alleged plot to carry out a violent attack on a Houston-area synagogue. Court documents reveal the suspects allegedly planned to drive a vehicle into a congregation with the stated goal of killing as many Jewish people as possible. The arrests come amid heightened concern over antisemitic violence following a recent attack on a Detroit-area synagogue.

item.title: EU Leaders Face Harsh Reality After €90B Loan Celebration
item.description: European Union leaders celebrated a landmark €90 billion loan agreement at a summit in the sun-soaked Cypriot resort of Ayia Napa, but the festivities were short-lived. As the champagne dried and the morning light crept in over the Mediterranean, the hard questions about repayment structures, political conditions, and long-term fiscal responsibility began to surface. What was sold as a moment of historic unity is now being scrutinized as one of the bloc's most complex and potentially divisive financial commitments in recent memory.

item.title: Judge Sets Hearing Date in D4vd Murder Case Over Teen's Death
item.description: A judge has set a hearing for next week after lawyers for singer D4vd demanded prosecutors publicly reveal their evidence in the killing and dismemberment of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez. The case has sent shockwaves through both the music industry and the broader public as details continue to emerge. Defense attorneys made their move Thursday, forcing a legal showdown over the strength of the prosecution's case.

item.title: 'The Rocky Horror Show' Broadway Review: A Time Warp
item.description: Broadway's long-awaited revival of 'The Rocky Horror Show' has arrived with a fiendishly talented cast led by Luke Evans, Rachel Dratch, and Juliette Lewis, and the result is nothing short of a theatrical revelation. Richard O'Brien's beloved cult classic finds new energy and irreverent joy in this bold new production that honors the source material while injecting fresh electricity into every sequined moment. This is the kind of show that reminds audiences why live theater remains an irreplaceable experience.

item.title: Jimmy Fallon Apologizes to Survivor's Christian Hubicki
item.description: Jimmy Fallon sat down with Survivor fan-favorite Christian Hubicki on The Tonight Show to personally apologize for his role in the elimination twist that ended the castaway's time in the game. Despite the unexpected ouster, Hubicki handled the moment with grace and humor, turning what could have been an awkward confrontation into one of late-night television's most charming exchanges of 2026. The two cleared the air in a conversation that has since gone viral across social media platforms.

item.title: Asia Stocks Slide as Tech Losses and Oil Surge Bite
item.description: Asian equity markets fell sharply on Saturday, April 25, 2026, as a broad sell-off in technology shares and a surge in global oil prices dampened investor confidence across the region. With traders already navigating a complex macroeconomic landscape, all eyes turned toward Japan's upcoming Consumer Price Index release, which many analysts believe could shape central bank policy for months ahead. The dual pressure of rising energy costs and faltering growth stocks created a challenging session for markets from Tokyo to Sydney.

item.title: U.S. Special Forces Soldier Arrested Over Polymarket Insider Bets
item.description: A U.S. special forces soldier has been arrested on charges of insider trading after allegedly placing bets on the prediction market platform Polymarket ahead of a classified military raid targeting Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro. Prosecutors say the case marks the first known criminal prosecution for insider trading conducted on a prediction market, breaking new legal ground in the rapidly expanding world of political and event-based wagering.

item.title: DeepSeek Unveils V4 AI Model, a Triumph for Chinese Chips
item.description: Chinese artificial intelligence startup DeepSeek has officially launched its long-awaited V4 model, sending ripples through the global AI industry. The release not only signals a major leap forward in Chinese AI capabilities but also represents a landmark achievement for China's domestic semiconductor industry, demonstrating that cutting-edge AI development is possible without relying on the world's most advanced Western-made chips.

item.title: Steelers Blindsided: Eagles Snagged Makai Lemon First in 2026 Draft
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers thought they had their man in wide receiver Makai Lemon during the 2026 NFL Draft, only to discover the Philadelphia Eagles had swooped in and selected him first. The mix-up, stemming from the league's truncated selection window, left Pittsburgh scrambling to adjust their draft strategy on the fly. It was a stunning moment that highlighted how razor-thin the margins are when teams compete for the same coveted prospects.

item.title: Steelers Blindsided: Eagles Swooped Makai Lemon Before Pick
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers believed wide receiver Makai Lemon was theirs for the taking in the 2026 NFL Draft, only to discover the Philadelphia Eagles had already snatched him off the board. The chaotic sequence unfolded during a compressed selection window that left Pittsburgh scrambling to adjust on the fly. It was one of the most dramatic behind-the-scenes moments of draft weekend.

item.title: Mike Vrabel's Hubris Made Him the 2026 NFL Draft's Main Character
item.description: The first round of the 2026 NFL Draft was widely panned as lacking energy and star power, but New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel found a way to inject himself into the national conversation. A tense interaction with a prominent NFL reporter and the resurgence of a simmering sex scandal turned Vrabel into the most talked-about figure of draft weekend.

item.title: US Eases Marijuana Restrictions in Major Win for Cannabis Industry
item.description: The United States Justice Department has officially reclassified state-regulated marijuana as a less dangerous controlled substance, marking one of the most significant shifts in federal drug policy in decades. The landmark decision is expected to unlock new financial opportunities for the legal cannabis industry, which has struggled under the weight of federal restrictions and banking barriers. Industry analysts say the move could trigger a wave of investment and consolidation across the sector.

item.title: Missing Scientists Spark Conspiracy Theories, Hurt Families
item.description: A growing number of deaths and disappearances among individuals connected to sensitive United States research programs have ignited fierce speculation online. While conspiracy theories spread rapidly across social media platforms, the families of those affected are pleading for compassion and truth. Experts warn that misinformation surrounding these cases is causing measurable harm to grieving loved ones.

item.title: Trump Eyes Taxpayer Takeover of Spirit Airlines at Right Price
item.description: President Donald Trump confirmed Thursday that his administration is actively considering a taxpayer-funded acquisition of Spirit Airlines, the embattled budget carrier that filed for bankruptcy protection in late 2024. Trump indicated the government would purchase the airline at the right price with the strategic intent of reselling it once oil prices fall. The proposal has already sparked significant debate among economists, aviation industry experts, and lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire Extended 3 Weeks After Trump Talks
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Sunday that the fragile Israel-Lebanon ceasefire has been extended by three weeks following a second round of intensive peace talks at the White House. The breakthrough comes amid mounting international pressure to prevent a renewed escalation of hostilities between the two nations. Diplomats on both sides described the extension as a critical window of opportunity to forge a more durable long-term agreement.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Calls for US-Iran Peace Talks, Condemns Death Penalty
item.description: Pope Leo XIV issued an urgent appeal Saturday for the United States and Iran to return to the negotiating table, calling for a peaceful resolution to the ongoing conflict between the two nations. Speaking aboard the papal plane returning from a historic trip to Africa, the pontiff also took a firm stance against capital punishment, calling it an affront to human dignity.

item.title: U.S. Soldier Accused of $400K Gain on Maduro Capture Bet
item.description: A U.S. Army soldier is under investigation for allegedly using classified intelligence to place lucrative bets on the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, netting approximately $400,000 through online prediction markets. The case has ignited a fierce debate about the intersection of national security and the rapidly expanding prediction market industry. Federal investigators are now examining whether the soldier exploited privileged military knowledge to gain an unfair advantage on platforms that allow users to wager on real-world geopolitical events.

item.title: Seahawks Select RB Jadarian Price With Final First-Round Pick
item.description: The Seattle Seahawks closed out the first round of the 2026 NFL Draft by selecting running back Jadarian Price with the final pick of the opening night. General Manager John Schneider had openly pursued a trade-down opportunity throughout the evening but ultimately could not find a willing partner before the clock ran out.

item.title: Matlock Boss Breaks Down Finale's Wellbrexa Wrap-Up
item.description: After two seasons of courtroom intrigue, Matlock finally delivered its long-awaited verdict in the Wellbrexa pharmaceutical case during a gripping Season 2 finale. The showrunner is now opening up about how that pivotal storyline wrapped up and what fans can expect from a dramatically reimagined Season 3, including a surprising time jump.

item.title: Eagles Earn Top Draft Grades for 2026 NFL First-Round Pick
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles walked away from the first round of the 2026 NFL Draft with their reputation as one of the league's shrewdest front offices firmly intact. General manager Howie Roseman and his staff drew widespread praise from analysts and draft experts for their selection. The pick is being viewed as a potential cornerstone addition to an already talented roster hungry for another championship run.

item.title: DOJ Watchdog Probes Epstein Act Compliance Failures
item.description: The Justice Department's internal watchdog has launched a formal investigation into whether federal agencies have fully complied with the Epstein Files Transparency Act, a landmark law requiring the release of documents related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The probe raises serious questions about transparency obligations and potential obstruction within federal bureaucracy. Officials and advocates are demanding answers about what records may still be withheld from the public.

item.title: Meet the 19-Metre Octopus That May Have Ruled Ancient Seas
item.description: New fossil evidence points to the existence of truly colossal octopuses that may have stretched up to 19 metres in length, prowling the ancient oceans more than 100 million years ago. Scientists believe these giants could have been among the most dominant predators of the Cretaceous seas. The discovery is reshaping our understanding of how complex and diverse marine life was long before modern creatures evolved.

item.title: US Reclassifies State-Licensed Marijuana as Less Dangerous Drug
item.description: The United States Justice Department has officially reclassified state-regulated marijuana as a less dangerous controlled substance, marking one of the most significant federal drug policy shifts in decades. The decision is expected to dramatically reshape the legal cannabis industry, which has faced persistent financial and regulatory headwinds despite widespread state-level legalization. Industry analysts say the move could unlock billions of dollars in economic activity and bring long-sought relief to cannabis businesses struggling under outdated federal restrictions.

item.title: 1 Dead, 9 Injured in Shooting at Mall of Louisiana
item.description: A violent shooting erupted at the Mall of Louisiana on Friday, April 24, 2026, leaving one person dead and nine others wounded in what authorities are calling a devastating incident. Two of the injured victims remain in critical condition as investigators work to piece together the events that led to the attack. Law enforcement flooded the scene as terrified shoppers scrambled for safety inside one of Baton Rouge's most popular retail destinations.

item.title: Xbox's New Chief Asha Sharma Reevaluates Exclusive Games Strategy
item.description: Microsoft's newly appointed Xbox chief Asha Sharma is shaking up the gaming division's long-standing approach to exclusive titles, signaling a significant strategic pivot for the brand. Sharma, who took the helm of Xbox earlier this year, has confirmed she is actively reevaluating which games should remain platform-exclusive and which should reach broader audiences. The move could fundamentally reshape the competitive landscape of console gaming as Microsoft looks to reestablish Xbox as a dominant force in the industry.
